mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as cost of capital falls.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts with p-value below 0.05, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of expansionary periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI increases above 0.4% trigger immediate equity sell-offs as investors anticipate restrictive monetary policy responses within the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, generating positive quarterly equity returns through wage-driven consumption and earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns with statistical significance at p-value below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series noise.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% annualized reflects solid macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP prints are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the predictive correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction that immediately depresses equity valuations over the following week during economic downturns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances during recessions, or the signal information coefficient remains negative across all rolling 2-year windows.
